reviews.review_title: Do You Want Fun, or a Challenge?
reviews.review_description:
First of all, let me say this. The title of this review is basically the whole point of the game: do you want fun, or do you want a challenge? Because that sums this game up perfectly. Do you want to play something fun, or do you want to play something challenging?

I’ve been playing VR since way back with the Oculus Go, so I was there from the beginning. I’ve played almost every big-name VR game, especially the popular ones, and this is only the second time I’ve ever made a comment or review. So I’m not someone who comments on everything.

And I can honestly say this: of every VR game I’ve ever played, Lies and Rain is hands down the best-looking VR game ever. When people think about VR, they imagine something like this. Some VR games look realistic, some look like Atari from the 1980s, but this game looks real. The graphics are no lie. This is what people fantasize VR is supposed to look like.

Of all the games I’ve played that are supposed to be futuristic, this is the one that actually feels like it came from the future. It feels like it is from a different time zone. The world, the art, the design, the atmosphere, the puzzles, the enemies, all of it is unique. You can tell a lot of creativity went into this game.

So with all that going for it, what’s the problem?

The problem is simple: it is not fun.

Or at least, it was not fun for me.

The biggest issue is the data worlds. Before I bought the game, I read a lot of comments, and one thing kept coming up: people hate the data worlds. I thought, “It can’t be as bad as people say.” No. It’s worse.

The data worlds look great. The world itself looks great. But they are annoying, and you have to complete them. You cannot skip them. They are mandatory. And they are one of the biggest reasons this game feels more like a task than something I actually want to play.

It is one of those things where, let’s say there are seven steps to finish it. You get to step six, then die before step seven, and now you have to start all over from the beginning. That is unbelievably annoying. You may have to do it dozens of times, and every time you have to repeat the same steps again. The game gives you no real leeway with that.

And what is the reward? You just move forward. That’s it. No power-up, no bonus, no nothing, no women, no nothing. You are getting nothing except permission to keep going.

That is what I mean when I say this game is challenging, but not fun.

The game is also very slow. I do not mean slow like nothing happens. There are enemies, and there is action. But it feels like you feel every inch you are taking just to get to the next plot point or the next area. It feels like an endless campaign of, sheesh, just trying to get somewhere.

The enemies are designed well. I like that they are unique and not just the same thing you see in every other game. But the action does not feel action-paced. It is more like an enemy comes along, you kill it, and then you keep going. Again, it feels more like duty than fun.

The puzzles are probably some of the most futuristic puzzles I’ve ever seen in VR. You really do feel like you are in a futuristic world. I’ve played a lot of puzzle games, and I’ve never seen puzzles quite like this. That is a good thing.

But they are also needlessly complicated. Because they are so new and different, you do not really have any basis for understanding how they work. With most puzzles, you can compare them to something you have seen before and say, “Okay, I think I know how to figure this out.” Not with these puzzles. With these puzzles, you are pretty much on your own trying to figure out what you are even supposed to do.

So not only do you have to solve the puzzle, first you have to figure out how the puzzle works. Maybe some people enjoy that. Maybe some people like that kind of trial and error. But to me, that was not fun. It was just another task.

And that is the strange thing about this game. Almost everything in it is unique. The graphics, the enemies, the puzzles, the world, all of it is unique. Normally that would be great. And in some ways, it is great. But in this case, everything is so challenging and unfamiliar that you cannot even focus on enjoying it.

There are other VR games that are slow or long-winded, but they are still fun. Metro VR, for example, has a lot of talking. I mean a lot of talking. But you honestly do not notice it as much because the action, the enemies, and the world are fun. Into the Radius can also be slow-paced, with you walking through a lot of emptiness, but it still feels fun and interesting.

With Lies and Rain, I kept feeling like I was trying to get through it instead of wanting to play it.

That is the frustration. This game is beautiful. It is creative. It is futuristic. There is nothing else currently out like it in VR. But just because something is unique and great-looking does not mean it is fun.

If you love a challenge, this game might be perfect for you. If you love figuring things out with very little help, dying over and over, repeating steps, dealing with complicated puzzles, and pushing through mandatory data worlds, then you may love this game.

But if you just want to have fun, zone out, and enjoy a VR game without feeling like you are doing homework from the future, this may not be the game for you.

For me, if they got rid of the data worlds, or at least made them skippable or less punishing, the game would be a lot more tolerable. Maybe even a lot more fun. But as it is, the game feels like a beautiful futuristic world trapped behind frustration.

So my honest review is this: Lies and Rain is one of the best-looking VR games ever made, maybe the best-looking one I’ve played. But it is also slow, monotonous, overly challenging, and often not fun.

Do you want fun, or do you want a challenge?

Because this game gives you the challenge.

And I wanted fun.

reviews.review_title: It's okay to think before shooting!
reviews.review_description: Phew! I'm exhausted. As the comfort level indicates, this game is absolutely "intense". It was almost too hard, at times frustratingly so, but I am glad I stuck with it because it's worth it. It feels like a spin off from Half-Life: Alex, which does not bother me at all. Graphics are awesome - dark, gritty, just as you'd expect in a post-apocalyptic world. Enemies are beatable, and yes, the data worlds are the most frustrating part of this and really a standalone game to a large degree. Once you finish the game and reflect on it, there was a lot of stuff going on and lots of fun. It's a shooter, but not a mindless one. You do have to think to not only get yourself through the various puzzles, but to strategize on how to best engage, counter, or altogether avoid the various enemies coming at you. Must play!

I recently picked up Of Lies and Rain and I just wanted to say that I think it’s a really solid and enjoyable VR experience. The atmosphere, gameplay, and overall concept are genuinely engaging, and it feels like a project made with a lot of care and passion.

I’m playing on Meta Quest using Quest Games Optimizer, and I have to say the game looks impressively sharp and runs quite well. The visual clarity and general performance are already at a very good level, especially for standalone VR.

That said, I wanted to offer a small piece of constructive feedback. The main area where I feel the game could improve significantly is the lighting. It’s not so much about adding advanced effects like volumetrics, but rather refining the overall lighting setup—how light interacts with the environment, shadows, and scene coherence. At times, the lighting can feel a bit inconsistent or flat, which slightly breaks the immersion.

I truly believe that with some adjustments to the lighting—whether through better baked lighting, improved contrast, or more cohesive light placement—the game could reach a near AAA-level visual quality on Meta Quest. The foundation is already there, and this kind of polish could make a big difference.

Overall, congratulations on creating a very enjoyable game. I wish you all the best moving forward, and I hope the project continues to grow and improve.

Thank you for your work!

This is a great game with very good physics. I see it was made by a small team but you really do see the passion they put into this. It's on par or better than most big budget titles. Plays very smooth on the Quest 3S on 90 FPS and I get some intense vertigo (in a good way) in the data worlds. I also like the story, world, and characters so far. Hits pretty hard with the AI mess of a world people keep trying to usher in. Highly recommend and wish we could have more games like this available.

Holds enough interest and playability to enjoy many hours of gameplay. 
Graphics are about an 7 out of 10 with QGO.
Nowhere near the best looking game in VR like some others have bizarrely suggested. Red Matter 2 still a few steps ahead.

Not enough creature variety. Guns feel weak and getting kills doesn't feel satisfying. The digital world sections are too frequent but.....not as frequent as having to read memos or data disks! OMG if I wanted to read so much I'd buy a book instead. 
Seriously waaaaay too much text to get through. I started dreading seeing another disk. 

All said, I enjoyed it. About a 6/10

Pay no mind to the reviews that trash the data worlds. I'd wager that those reviewers are of the ilk that tend to shy away from challenging situations in life. 

The data worlds are one of the best features of the game. The sheer scale alone is dizzyingly massive. As someone who actually appreciates challenge in video games, I found them difficult but not overwhelming. You'll fail and die a few times, but each one is possible to complete - even without any upgrades.

The rest of the game is amazing, too. The graphics are top-notch, the storyline is rich and nuanced, and gameplay is engaging. Brilliant game design from dedicated devs. Keep up the great work!

This might as well be half life Alyx for all I’m concerned. I loved the data world levels especially. Just wish there was an update to where you could play as a girl. That would be closer to half life Alyx in my opinion.

This is a pretty solid game. It has great physics-based puzzles and an excellent atmosphere. But the shooting aspect is very weak: most of the time you’ll be killing spiders and flying drones, and the core destruction sequence is so boring that you might end up uninstalling the game before finishing it.
Sometimes i get my eyes to the floor so i have to go on my knees and it fix the game but but its nice story

Data missions kill this game, couple that with the low frame rates, repetitive enemies, and dont get me started on the issues with the colors. Dont buy if you have any bit of colorblindness, or you will just walk into unseeable gas clouds, toxic floors......tried to be half life alyx, just replay half life alyx and skip this. 

It gets 3 stars because you can tell effort was put into it. Unlike a lot of the cynical cash grabs on this platform now.

reviews.review_title: I really wanted to like this game
reviews.review_description:
This game was heavily inspired by Halflife Alyx, and that's a good thing. Plenty of items to interact with, physics based puzzles, great looking graphics. Unfortunately the developer seems to have stopped around 75%.

Inconsistent and broken mechanics, getting attacked through walls, and the frame dropping. The almost constant stuttering is why I finally gave up, it's just not worth it. 

To the developer, why? Why do so many companies get so close and just ship? Do you just assume that we don't have anything else to play so we're stuck? I love the graphics but would much rather have smooth gameplay even if the graphics get turned down.

developer_response.body: You should try with different graphics settings, bring your framerate to 72 reprojected and you shouldn't ahve any problems. We tried so many different setting and that's the best one. It should keep a steady 72 fps.

reviews.review_title: Please release a non data world edition.
reviews.review_description:
Really wanted to complete it when bought but those data world missions are just not fun. I just want to complete the story but it gets bogged down with boring jumping puzzle levels with the worst physics ive ever seen. They are unskippable too and just not fun!
Don't get me started on that wack ending

I don't understand how this game can have such good physics, finger physics, story, combat and everything else. This game does everything right and I've loved it from the start. That fact that this is only 50bucks is just crazy. Please buy this game.
Brilliant from start to finish - Mercury Rain by Rustyfork in titles is a great song - fantastic work all involved

reviews.review_title: A great VR game for gamers
reviews.review_description: Disclaimer: I received the game for free for playtesting it, but I still bought and gifted a couple copies to support the devs. This is an Alyx-inspired VR game with its own personality and mechanics. It’s built from the ground up for VR, with physics puzzles and interactions that only make sense in VR – this is not something you could play on flatscreen. The game mixes a lot of elements: there’s platforming, shooting, puzzles, narrative, and even nostalgic callbacks. The target audience for this are the true gamers. While it's clearly inspired by Alyx, you can’t fairly compare it one-to-one because this is made by a small indie team of just 5 people and they did an amazing job. What you get is a full game with about 10 hours on a first playthrough, with exploration rewarded by hidden lore and worldbuilding. I absolutely recommend exploring the world and watching the story it tells you with the environment. Some dealbreakers to note: you should already be somewhat used to VR since this isn’t baby’s first VR game, it’s not ideal for people with motion sickness, and there’s very little hand-holding. Especially platforming sections some people found too difficult. There are no human NPCs or enemies, just the main character and a voice in your ear, and while most platforming can be skipped, some sections are mandatory. Right now on Meta you are getting only standalone version without the more superior PCVR which is available only on steam. Lastly, quick tip: the pistol, shotgun, and default data-world gun are all you really need if you use them smartly, and for upgrades I’d go triple jump for data worlds first and then laser for the pistol. I want this game to succeed, because I want more of this kind of VR in the future and I hope you can buy it and support the devs.

What a terrible game. It started off quite well, but then got worse and worse as time went on. Very poor gameplay, bad climbing mechanics, extreme balancing problems, so that you keep finding yourself in hopeless situations. The data worlds and skill puzzles are annoying and frustrating. In the tower, the game became so difficult that I just didn't feel like playing anymore. Oh, and the game is full of bugs. You constantly get stuck in walls, especially after those cable car rides.

reviews.review_title: Very impressive with the qgo app
reviews.review_description:
Well what can I say, I enjoyed half life Alyx and this game manages to pull of many of its feats.
Puzzles are nice (yes, I do enjoy the data worlds), physics are good and the enemies are beautifully made.
Interactions are good as well (opening doors, gun handling etc).
There is jank and some judder, but you can play around a lot with the settings.
The app Quest game optimizer can increase resolution by 200% and I've gotten decent performance when turning off asw (in game setting) and capping frame rate at 80 fps (also in game setting).
Is there jank? Sure, but the developers should be really proud of this.
I hope they break even eventually and will be able to make another game.

reviews.review_title: Good game with one big flaw
reviews.review_description: Data worlds are frustrating, not fun. You can skip most of them except for the boss battle. You have to stand on a small square and avoid being shot by about 10 different things. The square blends in with the background and if you fall off you start over from the beginning. If you die, it’s all the way back to the start of the level. Way to ruin a great game!

developer_response.body: Hi Bobo, you can actually move from the little box to avoid the shots at the last bossfight. please let us know if that doesn't wortk for you.

reviews.review_description:
There is a lot stuff in the game that makes it outstanding.
Great atmosphere and story. The enemies are somewhere between annoying and tough. I liked the idea that at some point you have to use their ability to remove obstacles. 

The data worlds are a real twist and it takes a while to get used to them. But then they are really fun to jump and fight through.

Overall i had a great time and can highly recommend this game.

It is so rare that games are this immersive, you can interact with almost anything. The gameplay and the story is super as well, it really captures the Sci fi apocalypse atmosphere. It's crazy that this game was done by a small team.
Small advise, it is worth to read the datapads you find, it gives a lot of details about the world and what happened to it.
For the devs, please continue to make games like this!

Just finished it. One of those you cannot put down until you complete it. Great story, amazing ambience, mechanics are incredible. The swinging between dataworld and real world really made it fresh for me, allowing this way for a little rest from the regular scenarios. Linear Game but very well done. Somebody reviewed that it feels like a copy of half life alix, well, you can’t find a better one to copy from than the best, so that should be a compliment…
Voice acting is great, and over all is a super fun game to play with a quite lengthy campaign. Advice, start collecting cubes from second 0, you’ll need all you can get. All the low reviews I’ve seen only had petty excuses for why they rated the game low. One said, I don’t know what to do, really?!anyway, 100% recommend

reviews.review_title: Was really good the first few hrs
reviews.review_description: Was enjoying the game the first few hrs, then it went heavy into the digital data world silly gymnastics nonsense 🙄 and some puzzles are very annoying like the circular sound piano 🎹 dail. Decided to quit had anuff of the digital world run a jump and die 10 times over and over. I'd got a refund if I could unfortunately.  Dissapointing later rounds of the game left a bad taste in my mouth, wish we could just bypass the gymnastics in the gamengive us a option since it makes no sense at all for the story.

The moment you step into Of Lies and Rain VR, you know you’re somewhere special. The first thing that hits you is the atmosphere, the rain feels almost alive, the air hums with tension, and every echo whispers fragments of a story you can’t quite grasp yet. It’s haunting, cinematic, and deeply immersive from the first second.

This isn’t just another VR shooter, it’s a psychological storm. Like Half-Life: Alyx, it pulls you into a world that feels tactile and real, where every movement and interaction matters. Yet its mood and tone lean closer to The Walking Dead: Saints & Sinners, enveloping you in a heavy, lived-in world full of decay, mystery, and consequence.

Where Alyx dazzled with polish and scale, Of Lies and Rain wins with atmosphere and storytelling. It doesn’t try to be a sprawling sandbox; instead, it focuses on tension, memory, and the uneasy line between digital illusion and human emotion. It’s more intimate, a narrative-driven experience that thrives on immersion and introspection.

Sure, it may not match the AAA refinement of Alyx or the survival systems of Saints & Sinners, but that’s not the point. Of Lies and Rain VR proves that powerful VR storytelling doesn’t need endless crafting menus or blockbuster budgets. It just needs heart, vision, and a world that pulls you in so completely you forget where you are.

When the rain falls around you and the lies begin to unravel, you’ll feel it, this is why VR exists.

🎮 Final Verdict:

A haunting, story-driven VR experience that stands proudly beside Half-Life: Alyx and Saints & Sinners, trading scale for soul, and winning.

reviews.review_title: Must Play, Diamond with few rough edges
reviews.review_description:
Truly amazing technical feat to have this running on Quest 3. 

The ambition of this game to be indie Alyx-clone is quite admirable, and it even succeeds in many aspects of it, but even though it fails to deliver on some, it manages to be its own thing a post-apocalyptic scifi, with deeply mysterious story and fun gunplay, that really excels at lot of other things than Alyx, more than making up for what it lacks.

The developers seem pretty open to feedback, especially on their discord, and they are actively improving the game, through the Early Access they've managed to quite chip away at this diamond in the rough. And even though some rough edges still remain, for me its the Game of the Year.

The gameplay is quite fun, tho some parts can take some getting used to, and even when it lacks the manual saving, it has quite good amount of autosaves, the only place where I really miss the saving is the data worlds.
At first I had mixed feelings about Data World levels, where the game turns into sort of a 3D platformer & shooter, it felt like the weakest part of the gameplay, but through the Early Access there was a lot of improvements, and it turned into one of the most fun parts of the game for me.

The story on first shallow playthrough is good, keeping quite a lot of mystery, narrated by intriguing radio dialogue with one person, plus bunch of texts to be found and read, so there is a lot of optional reading, if you want to get deeper into the story, but beware, through the reading you might discover the story is not just good, but truly masterfully written, keeping you guessing what are actually The Lies.

Overall it's an amazing game, that might seem just good, if you give it just shallow look, but it's really rewarding diving deep into it, it still has few issues, but nothing you cannot get over, and I believe most them will get ironed out in future updates.

We really need more VR games like this.

I didn't finish the game because I was unable to complete the final boss level in the Data world. I didn't enjoy the Data world element of the game generally and I found them frustrating because the menu screen kept popping up at random when I was in the middle of a jump. When I selected 'continue' I lost the jump and died. This happened frequently. Other glitches happened when picking up or reaching for items. I think this may be because I play seated and I expect it is less problematic when standing. In one section where there were the first exploding laser flower things, I kept finding myself sunk into the floor or elevated above the rooms looking down on what must be the developers viewpoint because I could see several rooms laid out ahead of me. I had to keep replaying this level because the glitch kept happening. I finally got through by standing up. The game was definitely value for money as it is long but I was disappointed not to finish. Also, the main actor's voice (Adam) was irritating as it sounded like had a bad case of constipation.  straining on the toilet.

reviews.review_title: This game should win a VR Award
reviews.review_description: This small team of devs did an incredible job. Just the fact that people compare their game to half Life Alex is a major compliment. It just needs the hit box improved.

reviews.review_title: One thing kills the whole game for me ..
reviews.review_description: If you were able to move faster than a snail covered in molasses , I would still be playing this one! The unbelievable slowwwwww walking speed and no ability to run or even jog made this game unplayable for me. I can't stand it. And it's too easy to get lost on what the mission is with no help or clarification whatsoever. Looks amazing visually, great story, but I completely stopped playing because of the slow movement. Not normal game slow before running. Like 3 to 4 times slower movement speed than any game I've played.

reviews.review_title: A little janky, but well worth a play!
reviews.review_description: Encountered few small issues, one quite annoying one near the end. Last boss was a giant pain.

Updated review (original below)
Finally finished after three days solid on the end boss/fight/world/thing... I stand by everything written below, I did all but two Data Worlds throughout the game and this last one was just too difficult because it wasn't clear at all what you were supposed to do, once I'd worked it out after three days, it was easy... I've read lots of reports saying the same thing, it's only easy after you've done it!

I have really enjoyed this game, I mean seriously really enjoyed it, discovered quite late on that the majority of the Data-World sections are not mandatory, so skipped the last two before the (I believe) ending, which is unfortunately far too hard, no not hard just frustrating, I've spent two days (doing pretty much nothing else) and not matter what I do I get right up to the Boss/thing and have nowhere left to go, can't shoot it and can't jump anywhere... I obviously understand that the final boss should be hard, but it should at least be understandable for the player what they are supposed to do (especially after 15 attempts), this is what I mean by "Not Hard, Just Frustrating" and when a game gets frustrating like this it leaves a bad taste in the mouth, especially after enjoying everything that had come before.
I appreciate I'll attract the "Just Git Gud" comments, but I have quite literally been as high as I can get as low as I can get and as far forward as I can get... Nothing left to jump on, can't shoot anything!!??!!

developer_response.body: We're sry you didn't find the way to finish the game, If you are where we think you are, you're right at the end of the game. Most people find that last dataworld too easy compared to other. We're sorry you'te getting frustrated, there are plenty of playthrough on youtube where you can see how to beat the whole game!

reviews.review_description: Game is brilliant. Gameplay is great. Not a whole lot of bugs to point out. Only issue is the graphics look awful!!! The title on the main screen is jaggy as all "get out" and everything is very pixelated and "muddy". Fortunately if you have Game Optimizer its easy to fix( 125% increase in resolution), but I CANNOT play this game without it. Also dark areas in this game are completely devoid of any textures. Even on full brightness dark areas are completely BLACK and really takes away from the immersion. All in all great game if you have game optimizer, but devs need to work on resolving the graphics and overall dark appearance. IMO. ....I could live with the darkness, but graphics....pretty please....with sugar on top! <3

The game is atmospheric, clearly inspired by titles like Half-Life and Tron.

The game is in Early Access, but it has everything it needs: a full story, upgrades, weapons, enemies, locations...

The developers did a great job, and the hard work and heart they put into it is evident in this game.
I played a few hours of this game and wow... The attention to detail is amazing. You can really tell love and passion was put into this game. A true must-play 👌👌

reviews.review_title: Fun and somewhat dark sci-fi adventure
reviews.review_description: Beautiful graphics and a good story. The game was challenging at times, and while I'm aware some of the platforming areas are optional, I made myself do them anyway. I bought the game in early access so many of the bugs I stumbled across are fixed now. Like Ark&Ade, there are coded messages I had fun decoding outside the game. I appreciate the save system which helped me out of a couple of jams of my own making. I plan to play this game again and see if I can do better on the platforming areas because I often got frustrated after many deaths and opted to just get through them without collecting everything I could.

The game is very reminiscent of Half-Life: Alyx, but in an indie version. It's very well-made, and I highly recommend playing it with a Meta Quest 3 optimizer like QGO. The story is good, the gameplay is excellent, it rarely crashes, and I didn't encounter any bugs. It's absolutely worth the asking price. I must already have over 10 hours of playtime, and I believe I'm still far from the end. 5 stars.

This game is a lot of fun! I got a copy for free to help with play testing. Even in early stages, this game was captivating. After they developed more features, it just kept getting better and better.  If you like story action like in Alien, Metro, and Behemoth, you're going to like this one! Devs were very receptive to feedback, making a polished experience. They will continue to listen if you share feedback with them.

reviews.review_title: This is how it's done! Awesome game!
reviews.review_description: Bought the game when it released in early access, came across some of those issues the dev mentioned. However,  even then the game was fun,  interesting,  and interactive.  The game came into early access with some polish! The devs for this game had constant and quick updates, not to mention open,  honest communication with the player base.  Asking and wanting to improve their product and doing it (within reason). More development teams should be like this.  I normally don't post,  but I have to give credit where credit is due and this game is great and the dev team being open,  honest,  and responsive, with quick updates has earned my respect and gratitude. I'm tired of coming across dead VR games that say they're in development but then the player base is ghosted for 6 months or longer,  no feedback, updates, or even just an honest were giving up on this project. I look forward to playing more of this game and any future games they create. Literally,  this is how early access should be done.

reviews.review_title: I love it! Just a few things though :)
reviews.review_description:
Let me first state, the game is impressive, runs well, cool visuals, story, weapons etc.

The game is 100% well made, there’s a couple things on the quest version that make it feel flat, mostly some textures, visual bugs, and a lack of shadows, whether dynamic or not, it makes it feel like you’re playing a quest 1 game.

Also the hand physics, the fingers they’re nice, I feel like its trying to beat the one’s from Batman AS vr, and you’re doing a good job! But they feel too rubbery, too wiggly, it breaks the immersion for me.

Don’t get me wrong I’m 100% enjoying this game, and it’s in early access so I’m sure the devs are working hard! And congrats on the release! 

And for my final point, I’ve noticed that a lot of the enjoyment seems to come from how familiar the game feels, almost like Half-Life: Alyx. While taking inspiration is fine, there are places where it feels closer to a direct copy. For example, some of the creatures don’t just share similar ideas — their animations and behaviors are nearly identical, with only their visual designs changed, almost like a different species of the same animal. Adding more originality here could really help the game establish its own identity. Also just completely copying the ammo from crates you break, the name gravity gun, and the gravity gloves, it’s cool but seeing as it all comes from the half life franchise, it doesn’t feel like inspiration, but copying.

I’ll end the negatives here. The game is great, cool mechanics, nice menu’s, nice idea with the gpu’s and getting upgrades with it, voice acting is nice, the visuals on the pc version are amazing! The quest cersion depends on which part of the visuals, but still impressive at times. The design of everything is very cool and well done, bravo.

Apologies for the big plate of negatives, there’s more positives to this game than negatives, I just think the negatives should be discussed more, so that this game may reach it’s full potential :)

So final word is, game is amazing, just a few things to fix, and what is for alot of people just  similar, feels like a copy to me, but people are enjoying it, so congrats!

Cant wait to play more, especially when its out of early access!!

reviews.review_title: AAA from a small, big hitting team
reviews.review_description:
Wonderful experience, polished and VR-tastic. This doesn’t feel like something a small team would make , it feels like a blockbuster. The world is satisfying, the mechanics are great and it’s so built for VR it’s untrue.

Hand over your cash this studio deserves support, and you’ll love it

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot Dave!!! We're doing our best!

reviews.review_description:
This game has that chunky tactile feel i love when playing vr. All the equipment, actions and gameplay mechanics feel great inside the headset. The demo did s great job conveying this destroyed world, but man, there is so much more cool stuff to see in the full version. 

If your unsure of picking this one up, just go try the demo first. You'll be hooked!

Disclaimer: I have been playtesting this game from the early stages. I'll recieve the game for free but I'm also purchasing 4 copies to give away.

This is absolutely outstanding game, from the first 3 hour demo, to this amazing work of art. This is without a doubt highly influenced by Alyx, but the way it’s brought to be its own as well. I’ve been completely blown away by how great this game is, I’ve already played 4 hours of it. No idea where that time went so fast. Wait a minute, I better not have been probed. 
   Thank you very much for this masterpiece, I can very much see the blood, sweat, and tears, that you have clearly put into this game. Can’t wait to see what you do next. Don’t give into those strictly multiplayer trash heeps though. YOU NEVER GO FULL MULTIPLAYER! However; this is worth the price tag.
    Updated review. After the last 3 updates. My game is stuck trying to render the graphics, it sucks  because I’m right at the end. It’s now so fuzzy looking it gives me a migraine, every time I try to play now. Please fix this, it’s now at a point of being completely unplayable.

developer_response.body: Where is it stuck at? can you go over your graphics option is it 72 hz with spacewarp? wait did you upload you quest to version 81 ? it is giving a lot of problems so if you can chose don't update, i'm sure they'll fix the issues with version 82.

reviews.review_description:
Just finished the game, so updating the review. Loved the story, the graphics are crisp, weapon handling feels spot on and I feel fully immersed when in this world. I recommend reading the memories and the documents you find, it adds to the story. I didn't want the game to end. 

I think the devs added some really unique elements to the game while including some familiar homages to other games and movies.

I enjoyed the dataworlds but I would have made more progress in them as I didn't appropriately upgrade my gravity gun or switch to the machine gun when needed.

 The final boss was a nice added touch. Cool end game scenario imo 

Great jobs devs. This one was a very satisfying purchase. Up there with enter the radius and saints and sinners 1 for me.

Where to begin? First off, I can't believe this was made by a small team of 5 people. I honestly don't know why this is considered early access, its hands down better than most games I've played from AAA devs. Graphically beautiful from the opening, inventive puzzles (I especially love the crowbar/locker one). Even the data worlds are fun, really challenging for sure, but thrilling just getting out alive. Exploring every nook and cranny looking for the cubes to upgrade keeps things fresh when you get to enhance yourself or a weapon. I appreciate the lack of hand holding as well, problem solving on the fly is super satisfying. Good story told though radio communications and data disks found around really help build the world. This game probably won't be for everyone, but if you're looking for a challenging Sci fi adventure, this is well worth a buy. We need more devs like these guys!!!
    
One critique though, some sections are a little too dark, especially towards the mid/end point of the game. And what's the deal with the chairs in the offices? They must have been made for giants lol. 

Seriously though if the devs see this, amazing work guys! This is game of the year material!!

developer_response.body: We saw this!!! Thanks a lot!!! thoose chaird are giving us a lot of problems really! :) Thanks a lot for playing our game!

reviews.review_description:
I’ve been playing Of Lies and Rain since last year as a playtester, and watching its evolution has been impressive. Developed by a small indie team of just five people, the game punches far above its weight. The atmosphere is dense and oppressive, with world-building that feels lived-in rather than staged. Every ruined structure and every echo in the distance contributes to a sense of place that rivals what I’ve experienced in the biggest AAA VR titles.

The mechanics stand out as much as the presentation. Traversal, combat, and environmental puzzles flow together naturally, rewarding attention and precision instead of cheap spectacle. Switching between the physical world and its digital counterpart never feels gimmicky—it creates meaningful variety and keeps encounters fresh. Weapons have real weight, enemies force you to think strategically, and the pacing balances exploration with tense firefights in a way few VR shooters manage.

Verdict: Of Lies and Rain is one of the rare indie VR projects that truly feels AAA. It’s built with care, polish, and a clear vision that shows in every detail. If you’re an experienced gamer looking for a story-driven shooter that respects your time and skill, this is a must-play.

I am already 9 hours in the game and i am only in two thirds of progression. I am totally loving it! Inspiration with HL Alyx is definitely there but is not just a cheap copy. It only used some of the mechanics but all the enemies and environment are unique and fun. Graphics are total masterpiece pushing Q3 to its limits (with QGO). There is some stuterring during loadings but nothing terrible. I fully recommend this game. It is a gem.

reviews.review_title: The lengthy VR story shooter i need
reviews.review_description:
(i already posted this review to the discord, which i recommend you join, im just writing it here too so this gets boosted. More people need to play this) 

So i finally played through the game...

Holy bang doodle did i love the story.

This game scratched that "good lengthy single player story shooter" itch ive been having for A WHILE now. 

It felt like i was in some sci fi book, and i mean that in the best way possible.

Just one small thing...

The ending (to me atleast) feels extremely open, and i mean that more in a "this feels like a cliffhanger" than just a normal open ending. Honestly, i would love a sequel, or even a prequel (one where you would play as sam?) all in all, i love this, i need more. As that one man in that one meme once said


"Faboulous give me 14 of em right now" 

10/10 for me. Even with its flaws, the slightly frustrated parts it sometimes has (my skill issue probably lol) spiders (arachnophobe talking here lol) and other stuff, the story and the world  genuinely is just great. Please, if yall can and want to, i need more in this world. 

(im begging you plz)

I have been waiting for this game for a while. Then I found the demo and was REALLY excited. It was GREAT. I enjoyed the story. The graphics were awesome & it was super long. I kept thinking it has to be close to the end. It might be the longest campaign game I've ever played. I loved it. I am wondering why it's early access. There were no bugs, it ran really smooth. It was really long (again, a good thing). The ending did sort of leave it open...is there more to the ending? Or a part 2? Either way, I thoroughly enjoyed it. Awesome game.

I absolutely loved Of Lies and Rain! This game kept me on my toes from start to finish. Incredibly suspenseful, unpredictable, and totally entertaining. The story pulls you in right away and doesn’t let go.

The only part that threw me a bit was going into the Data World. If you have a fear of heights then you’ll definitely get over it fast! Those sections weren’t my favorite but thankfully, you didn’t have to complete most of them to progress and at the very end when you face the final boss!

The weapons were awesome, the pacing was great, and I was completely immersed the entire time. I’m already looking forward to Of Lies and Rain 2! Bring it on! 😄

Well done and bravo Castello Inc! M~

The amount of work that’s gone into this early access game is incredible! So much thought and planning for a complex story with many parallels to today’s world. And it’s looong! I have thought I was at the final boss a couple of times and it turned out there was more. I truly can’t wait to see the final production release. It fun to play now and I recommend d you get in when it’s inexpensive. When the dev team irons out all the tiny bugs and nails the lighting and shaders this will be a true gem!

developer_response.body: We're working on it! Final release will be out soon!

reviews.review_title: A VR game that was properly developed an
reviews.review_description:
It’s an apocalyptic adventure game with a captivating story.
I think I played a game that was properly developed and released, not a sloppy VR game that was released halfway through.
I really enjoyed the game because it had various interactions and a solid, well-structured composition.

After spending two and a half hours with Castello Studio's early access VR shooter Of Lies and Rain, I'm not ready to deliver a full review, but there's enough here to warrant attention. This Quest and PC VR title wears its Half-Life: Alyx influences openly - from physics systems and gravity glove mechanics to enemy designs that feel very familiar.

The pacing is deliberately slow, building foreboding and mystery, though after two hours I found myself craving more action. The lack of a sprint button became increasingly frustrating when exploring larger areas. However, the physics system impresses, especially on Quest - nearly everything reacts believably, and object interactions feel natural if not quite as polished as Valve's benchmark.

Combat is functional rather than revolutionary, with enemy designs reminiscent of Alyx's barnacles and face-huggers. Visually, the game opts for clean, neon-tinged sci-fi over gritty realism. Some stretches even reminded me of Metro Awakening, creating what feels like an ambitious indie mashup held together with perhaps a little too much duct tape - at the moment.

On Quest, the visuals are surprisingly solid for a small team, with lighting doing heavy lifting for atmosphere, though occasional flat textures break immersion. Audio follows a similar pattern - lovely environmental touches like rainfall changing tone under tin roofs, but weak weapon sounds and forgettable ambient music.

This is a slow burn that knows what it wants to be, derivative as that may be. For players who prefer unhurried exploration over constant action, it could be rewarding even now. I'd recommend wishlisting at minimum.

For our full impressions, search YouTube for 6DOF Reviews Of Lies and Rain!

reviews.review_title: This is one heck of a thrilling game
reviews.review_description:
Of Lies and Rain is finally here !! (I played the demo)

This game is giving me Remnant of the Ashes vibe as the drones kindof look like the Roots! The game is a first-person shooter with you playing as a data human. A drone race invades the earth and so far, seems like there’s no human survivors but you’re guided by someone through a headset. You use these GPU cube like objects to upgrade your abilities and weapons. The game is visually stunning and immersive with horrifying scenes of bloody dead bodies, brief encounters with the large alien droids but there are some minor flaws. 

Picking up objects feel very “weightless” and have no actual use, like knives and tools found randmly throughout the map. The experience in the “data world” is a bit underwhelming as theres these large 2D orb “enemies” that you shoot but they dont interact with you at all. Bit confused of its purpose in the game but still much more to explore! Do wish there were puzzles or more interactions on another note.

Am also a bit concerned of the lack of help or guidance in progressing through the campaign. You’re really left on your own to figure out how to get past checkpoints. For example, when underground you have to use and place a propane tank next to the digital looking rocks that are blocking the path and blow it up by shooting at it. That idea came naturatly to i from avid gaming experience but may not be so intuitive for casual gamers. 

Im still way early in the game but so far it’s keeping me on the edge of my seat!

When I first purchased this game I really didn’t know what to expect. The trailer looked cool but I had no clue how truly fantastic this game would progress. I have a RotoVR chair so it seriously adds to the experience but this game was like the matrix meets half life and just wowed the hell out of me. Jumping through the digital world was insane and really pushed my fear of heights to the limit but in the best way. My adrenaline was going up and down. The story was fantastic. I loved this game and recommend it to everyone. Especially if you have one of those new 360 gaming chairs. Wow!!!!

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot! Glad you liked the game!

reviews.review_description: For those of us who aren't regular gamers this game may not be for you.   There is NO tutorial, I have no idea what to do other than walk around and open doors....etc.  I found an area where you can upgrade/get items in exchange for the cubes you collect. Although I still have no idea how to get a gun.  I hate this game and can't get a refund as I used credits, which is my fault.  So I guess I'll just go walk  around opening doors and drawers. So disappointed.

developer_response.body:
Yeah the game doesn't tell you exactly what to do, we went instead for letting people explore and dive in the game. You can watch a lot of videos on YouTube to understand how to go through the most difficult parts.
This is a full playthrough, https://youtu.be/8A1C3VRUOZk?si=ljkXG0D9DFlGQ0mN if you need any more help please let us know

reviews.review_description: Everything about this game is superb. Even when I have died numerous times, working out how to get through certain areas is intriguing. One of the best games I've played in VR since HLA. We'll done devs

reviews.review_title: The kind of game I enjoy
reviews.review_description: Despite repeatedly humble claims that this was made by a small, inexperienced dev team, I think they've managed to craft something in Of Lies and Rain even a large well funded team could be proud of.  There's a lot to love here.  Its a full fledged narrative campaign with some interesting gameplay mechanics, high quality set piece visuals, and all tied together with a surprisingly robust physics system, a rarity still amongst campaign games and one of my favorite parts here.  Most 'clutter' objects are interactable, and there's some nice organically integrated ways to use them like breaking glass, tossing explosive cannisters into groups of enemies, or even holding an object up as a shield. I hope to one day see interactions like this become the standard for all VR games.  For an early access game, its solid enough already and any gripes or bugs I've encountered, the devs have already been working on improving. You can even play the demo, called 'Of Lies and Rain Demo' if you're still on the fence about it.  This is exactly the kind of game I enjoy from VR and hopefully we get to see more from these devs.
Great atmosphere, solid mechanics and the story is mysterious yet intriguing.  These indie Devs created something special here.  Must play!!!!

This game keeps blowing my mind over and over AND over again! Anyone who has given this AMAZING EARLY ACCESS game a bad review isn't a real gamer and doesn't see the genius and hard work and mechanics of this game. I've said it and will stand by it. Games like this are why I have a VR. Adventure, mystery, and story with amazing voice actors, AMAZING GRAPHICS, actual length to it, hidden treasures, and some of the coolest mechanics in a game I've seen since Asgard's Wrath 2! Just look at the healing needle when you go to use it! It looks so cool! I love replaying games like this after a while once I beat them so I can't wait to try the finished product after it's done. I'm not even done with the early access yet!!! 

Ps.Girl, if you don't add a run button and let me sprint lol I'm dying at this slow pace you got me stuck at

developer_response.body: Sprint button is coming in the next update!!! We got you covered.

reviews.review_description: enemies are lame.  guns are junky.  super easy. bad graphics low frame rate

reviews.review_title: Played less than 2 hours, LOVE IT!!
reviews.review_description:
These developers did a great job. The physics, environment, gun mechanics, and overall story are all top tier for Quest in my experience (I've never used PCVR, its all Quest for me since im on the road all the time for work).
I know I'm going to absolutely love this game and I'm only 2 hours in. The developers should be compensated greatly so they can continue to make VR games like this 🤟

Played thru 3 times, so far. 1st time was challenging, 2nd & 3rd time a blast because I understood better what upgrades to get and mechanics. 

Looking forward to forward to the sequel!🙏🤞
Great game! The story is engaging and entertaining. I highly recommend it if you’re looking for a good, interesting story to follow — or to play through yourself.
Positive reviews are right! Great game, great mechanics, good difficulty, nice upgrade system. Recommended!
All I can say is get this game and support the devs! I have been looking forward to this since I played the demo, and the full game did not disappoint. This game should get a 5.0!
Really great game but the jumping stages are just a pain in the ***. Had to give up in the end as I couldn't do them. Such a shame as the game is excellent 😕

developer_response.body: Those part are a bit difficult but most of them are not mandatory and you can skip them. Tip: Get the 3 jump upgrade and keep in mind that to reset the jump you just need to touch the cube, not landing on them.

reviews.review_description: So I get to this point where the guy is walking around asking about the roots and the girl says she doesn’t know if they’re alive but they’re certainly thriving and the guy gets to an elevator and there’s a button to push and he pushes the button is the player and nothing happens and I’m just standing there trying to figure out if this is a bug or if I’m doing something wrong, but there’s literally nothing else to do but push this button so guys????????????????

developer_response.body: Not a bug, you need to put the red wheel in its spot and solve the puzzle that activates the elevator. Let us know you solve it.

reviews.review_description: Every 5 minute you play come the stupid puzzle of jumping and big worm chase you these game have no content only climp crowl jump good graphic but game core bad

reviews.review_title: When is this coming out of early access?
reviews.review_description:
I bought this when it first came out on early access

When is this coming out of early access?

developer_response.body: November 4th, 4 pm GMT.

reviews.review_description:
Loving this game so far but it does have some bugs (why do I still see lightning flashing inside with no windows lol) and framerate drops here and there but this is in early access so those issues should get ironed out. The gameplay itself is some of the best Ive seen in VR, definitely recommended

Edit #1 - man this game gets intense, still loving it but I kinda wish the breathing masks were 1min instead of 30 seconds (yes you can upgrade to 1min just wish that was the base lol), more than once I've had to just run and hope for the best as I'd run out of masks. I think I'm closing in on the end now and still say if your into this type of game you should definitely buy it

Edit #2 - Ok, this game has some intense difficulty on some of the data worlds and door unlocks but luckily these seem to be avoidable if their too much for you (I cant do them lol) and you really got to pay attention to see where you need to go (got lost for awhile lol)

Edit #3 - after getting lost and giving up for a bit, I return and sure enough the room i avoided is where I needed to go, luckily this time I was able not to get stuck in the chairs and I'm in a pretty cool scene 😎 and even better the footsteps patch is in which I didnt really mind it before but it does add that extra polish.

Now that I've beaten the game i can say other than a few rough edges (They've already fixed some) and some gamer error 😆 this game was amazing. 

In response to dev - 
Ok I do understand you can stop in the middle but with the spider theres no time to do that really. I do apologize that I was sure one of these had to be the way to go tho as it was definitely frustrating as I was into the story and then sorry thats it. But seems it was just me 😆 I've returned my score to 5 🌟 sorry for my mistake on what I was supposed to do.

Ahh its the room where I kept getting stuck on the chairs so I avoided it 😆 and that next Data Gate was fun.

developer_response.body:
Hi Jakenut!

Yeah the game gets really intense later on, you can watch a lot of videos on YouTube to understand how to go through the most difficult parts.
This is a full playthrough, https://youtu.be/8A1C3VRUOZk?si=ljkXG0D9DFlGQ0mN if you need any more help please let us know

I understood where you are in the game, but those door and dataworld are not mandatory. That's not the way you need to go. You just need to explore more and you'll find out that you have to climb out a window to proceed in a room doesn;t need any puzzle to solve.

To solve that puzzle you just need to stop in the middle of every spinning bit. you can stand still there and solve one bit at a time. Anyway we've taken your feedback in account and we'll modify that puzzle even if it's not mandatory, it leads to a room that is not game blocking.

reviews.review_description: If you've never played half life Alex this game is about as close as you're going to get to it without buying a gaming PC and hooking up. Most of the mechanics are extremely similar even the vibe is extremely similar. The gravity gloves are excellent in this game the Gunplay is excellent the graphics are excellent everything in this game just screams premium. If you look at where these developers have come from it's like they went from a go-kart to Corvette in one iteration. They went from we're going to try this VR thing to we're in this 100%. I can't wait to see where this game goes and where these developers end up as a team. Anyway do yourself a favor and purchase this game.

developer_response.body: ahahhaa "go-kart to Corvette" just amazing, thanks a lot! We learned a lot during our years of gamedev. We're glad you're enjoying our game! Thanks a lot!

reviews.review_description: Saw this and watch trailer then had to get it as feels like HL Alyx etc only gripe I got don’t know if anybody else has the same but goes a bit jankie when moving ? But still great thanks Devs

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review!!

reviews.review_description: I’m blown away at the level of polish this game on quest 3 has it’s undeniable that you have truly got a future classic 10/10

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot!!! Hopefully we'll get to the vr classic one day! :)

reviews.review_title: Great game but MAJOR BUG ISSUES
reviews.review_description: As of right now, I love this game BUT I just had a few bugs happen to me not this long ago. What I just went through was all of my ammo and supplies have vanished, game has been spawning me in random areas that I haven't explored yet, and ammo and memories aren't being saved up behind my shoulder.
I have not actually played the game. I will update my star review when I play it in full release. Just wanted to support the team. ￼

developer_response.body: Love you !

reviews.review_description: An absolute dream! I don't know what else to say about this title. Everything is so intense and brilliantly executed. A definite must-have!

reviews.review_title: Of lies and rain is a great game...
reviews.review_description: that last update did something negative to the graphics.

Devs are very receptive and kind and listen to the playerbase.  

Graphics are pretty good for a q3, very nice atmosphere and story.  Good physics.

DONT MISS OUT!

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for your kind words! We're doing our best to listen to player feedback! We'll update once a week untill final release! We want to make it the best it can be.

reviews.review_title: A spiritual successor the HL Alyx
reviews.review_description: This game is very heavily influenced by Half-life Alyx, and in a good way. The upgrade system, gun selection mechanics, and even the gravity gloves, are all reminiscent of Alyx. But the story is definitely good on its own, and worthy of those mechanics. Its kinda like a mix of HL Alyx and Johnny Nemonic. The graphics are also really good, even for stand alone. I've played this on both PCVR and Standalone, and Id say that the standalone version is comparatively good. The only thing I thought was hands down better in the PCVR version, is the look of the data world. Its a little lacking in the standalone version by comparison, but probably had to be to make it playable. Anyways, overall Id give it an 8/10. Highly recommended.
The game is absolutely awesome from start to finish.

developer_response.body: You're awesome!!!

reviews.review_title: One of the best VR games I've played.
reviews.review_description: Of Lies and Rain is hands down one of the best VR games I've ever played. I was highly anticipating it after playing the demo a while back and am seriously impressed. Everything from the graphics to the story to the guns is amazing! Genuine 10/10, BUY ITTT!!
It is not working now   after playing many days

developer_response.body:
Sorry for the issue, we've uploaded a faulty build.
It should be fixed now, could you please retry?
Thanks for your report
